summary: A unit test failed during the hibernate-core test run: DbVersionTest.testCollectionVersion failed an assertion about owner version incrementing, causing the :hibernate-core:test Gradle task to fail.
root_cause: The test DbVersionTest.testCollectionVersion failed with an assertion indicating the owner's version was incremented when the test expected it not to be.

evidence.reason: Shows which test failed and that it failed during the Gradle test execution.

evidence.quote: org.opentest4j.AssertionFailedError: owner version not incremented ==> expected: <false> but was: <true>
evidence.reason: The assertion failure message explains the exact mismatch: the test expected the owner version not to be incremented but it was.
Indicates the Gradle test task for the hibernate-core module failed as a result of the failing test.

evidence.quote: There were failing tests. See the report at: file:///home/runner/work/hibernate-orm/hibernate-orm/hibernate-core/target/reports/tests/test/index.html
evidence.reason: Points to the test report that contains details about the failing test run.

suggested_fix: Reproduce and inspect the failing test locally (for example run the single test via Gradle) to determine why the owner's version is being incremented. Check the code paths that modify collection and owner versioning for DbVersionTest.testCollectionVersion, update the code to avoid unintended version increments or update the test expectation if the intended behavior changed. Review the detailed test report at the referenced report path to get stack traces and context.
